vitals.date: 2026: Cancelled (one-year hiatus; organizer states a 2027 return, no date confirmed yet)
vitals.date_specific: 2026: Cancelled (one-year hiatus; organizer states a 2027 return, no date confirmed yet)
vitals.entry_fee: 2025 pricing (last held edition): $85 Piccolo / $140 Medio / $160 Gran / $260 Gran-X

terrain.surface: Mixed paved roads and hard-packed dirt (roughly 18 of 109 miles unpaved on the flagship Gran per organizer figures)

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 70. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 66.

course_description.character: Four route options starting and finishing at Bristol Rec Park in Bristol, Vermont, using different combinations of the LAMB gaps — Lincoln, Appalachian, Middlebury, Brandon — that cut through Vermont's Green Mountains: Piccolo (40 mi) climbs no gap at all, Medio (80 mi) crosses two (Middlebury, Brandon), the flagship Gran (roughly 109 miles, ~9,680 ft climbing, also cited as 9,850+ ft in the organizer's own overview) runs clockwise over four (Appalachian, Lincoln, Middlebury, Brandon), and the Gran-X (roughly 149 miles, ~15,090 ft) adds Roxbury and Rochester Gaps for six gaps total in a figure-eight layout. None of the four routes is fully paved: the organizer's detailed course data lists roughly 10 dirt miles each on Piccolo and Medio, 18 on the Gran, and 21.5 on the Gran-X, and the FAQ recommends road or gravel bikes with at least 28-30mm tires.
course_description.signature_challenge: The four-gap sequence on the Gran — Appalachian, then Lincoln, Middlebury, and Brandon — back to back over roughly 109 miles.
suffering_zones: Appalachian Gap — the first gap on the Gran route, setting the tone early rather than serving as a warm-up
Lincoln Gap — the steep east-side pitch, roughly 1.4 miles per the organizer's detailed climb data, not a 7-mile ascent as previously described here
Middlebury Gap — roughly 7 miles long per the Gran-X climb table, arriving with real accumulated fatigue
Brandon Gap — the closing gap on the Gran, the last significant climb before the return to Bristol

history.origin_story: Launched in 2014 (planning began in 2013) starting at the Middlebury College Snow Bowl in Hancock, with 181 registrants and 160 starters at the inaugural edition. Originally produced by Cycle Addison County, Inc.; Todd Warnock's Ride It If You Can LLC took over direction in 2018 and moved the start/finish to Bristol Rec Park in 2019, where it has run since. Became a partner event of the Gran Fondo National Series in 2022.

biased_opinion.summary: The Vermont Gran Fondo is an organized mass-participation ride through the LAMB gaps of Vermont's Green Mountains, held annually since 2014, under Todd Warnock's direction since 2018 and based in Bristol since 2019 (current organizer: Ride It If You Can LLC). It is explicitly non-competitive except for timed gap-climb segments — there's no racing license requirement and no overall finish-order competition. The organizer's own site confirms a hiatus for 2026 ('We're Taking a Break in 2026 — See You in 2027'), though no 2027 date or registration has been published yet. The 2025 edition was held in Bristol on June 28, drawing 371 registrations across four routes.
strengths: Four genuine LAMB-gap climbs (Appalachian, Lincoln, Middlebury, Brandon) across its routes, plus two more (Roxbury, Rochester) on the Gran-X
Four distance options (Piccolo/Medio/Gran/Gran-X) covering a wide range of rider ambition, from a no-gap 40-mile ride to a 149-mile, six-gap route
A decade-plus track record dating to 2014, with the current organizer (Todd Warnock/Ride It If You Can LLC) running it since 2018
Explicitly non-competitive format for most of the field, reducing the pressure of a full-blown race for riders who just want the climbs
weaknesses: No 2026 edition — the organizer has taken a confirmed one-year break with no locked-in 2027 date yet, so this is not currently a bookable event

Course is not fully paved despite the 'road' framing: roughly 10 dirt miles on Piccolo/Medio, 18 on the Gran, and 21.5 on the Gran-X, per the organizer's own detailed figures — riders on typical narrow road tires are below the FAQ's recommended 28-30mm minimum
Organizer's own published stats are internally inconsistent between the narrative overview and the detailed route data (9,680 ft vs. 9,850+ ft on the Gran, with a similar gap reported on the Gran-X)
Registration count is modest for a four-route event (371 in 2025) — this is a regional ride, not a marquee national fondo
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A well-established Green Mountains gap-climbing ride with a confirmed hiatus for 2026. Worth tracking for a 2027 return if you want the LAMB gaps in one supported day, but there's nothing to register for right now.

final_verdict.one_liner: Vermont's LAMB gaps in one supported day — currently on a confirmed one-year hiatus.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Not in 2026 — there's no edition to enter. If it returns in 2027 as the organizer intends, it's a solid regional option for riders who want Appalachian, Lincoln, Middlebury, and Brandon Gaps in a single supported ride, on gravel-capable tires.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a similar Vermont/New England gap-climbing ride while this is on hiatus, look at other Green Mountain National Forest-area gran fondos and gravel events that use the same gap network.

videos.title: Vermont Gran Fondo - Lincoln Gap 2016 edit
videos.description: Our new video of famed Lincoln Gap with grades reaching 24%; named tin the steepest paved mile in the US by Bicycling magazine. The 2016 event course moves the notorious climb from the second climb position of 2015 to the final position for 2016.
videos.curation_reason: Course preview showcasing the notorious Lincoln Gap climb with 24% grades, positioned as the final challenge on the 2016 course.

videos.title: 2017 Vermont Gran Fondo
videos.description: With heavy rain in the forecast, I opted for the Medio Fondo 68 mile route.  I made it over Appalachian Gap and Lincoln Gap before the rain started. I made it up Lincoln without stopping or walking on the max 24% grade, which was my goal.  Great after party at Woodchuck Cider.
videos.curation_reason: First-person race recap with specific details about conquering Lincoln Gap's 24% grade and racing strategy in bad weather conditions.

videos.title: Vermont Gran Fondo - Are You Up To The Challenge
videos.description: The Vermont Gran Fondo is produced by Cycle Addison County, Inc. The organizing committee is made up of cycling enthusiasts and athletically-inclined tourism and business development representatives. Event proceeds will be distributed via grants to municipalities and non-profit organizations with the purpose of enhancing bicycling in Addison County through programs, education, infrastructure and/or equipment.

rider_intel.search_text: The provided transcript does not contain information about the Vermont Gran Fondo race. The video appears to be a casual bike ride in the Brattleboro area with a rider testing GoPro equipment and climbing a mountain, but contains no specific details about the Vermont Gran Fondo course, conditions, challenges, or race experience. No actionable intelligence about the 62-mile gravel race in Stowe, Vermont can be extracted from this content.

eligibility.notes: The organizer's own site states: 'We're Taking a Break in 2026 — See You in 2027.' This is a confirmed one-year hiatus, not a permanent discontinuation — no official 2027 date has been published yet, and third-party listings showing a June 2027 date should be treated as provisional. Last completed edition: June 28, 2025, Bristol, Vermont, 371 registered riders across four routes.

catalog_flags.status_note: Corrected this wave: location was wrongly recorded as Stowe, VT (a stale third-party 'Vermont Grand Fondo' calendar mirror places the event in Stowe under a misspelled name and appears to be the likely source of the contamination, though causation isn't provable) — current start/finish is Bristol Rec Park, Bristol, VT, the venue since 2019 (the inaugural 2014 edition was based at Middlebury College Snow Bowl in Hancock, not Bristol). Vitals (99.8km/1,463m) were also stale, matching a 2018 'Medio Facile' intermediate course rather than the flagship Gran (109mi/175km, ~9,680ft/2,950m); corrected to the flagship Gran figures. Founding year corrected from 2016 to 2014. Organizer corrected from Cycle Addison County (original 2014-2018 producer) to current organizer Ride It If You Can LLC (Todd Warnock, director since 2018).
